  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: Grocery service capabilities (pharmacy, fuel center, floral, MFC) are frequently operated under vendor partnership contracts. Linking service_capability to supplier enables vendor contract management,']
  activation_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the service capability at this store location. Active means the service is currently available to customers.']
  average_fulfillment_time_minutes varchar [note: 'Average time in minutes required to fulfill a customer order or transaction using this service capability. Used for customer communication and capacity planning.']
  capability_category varchar [note: 'High-level category grouping for the service capability to support filtering and reporting.']
  capability_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code identifying the type of service capability (e.g., CURBSIDE_PICKUP, HOME_DELIVERY, PHARMACY_DRIVE_THRU, COINSTAR, WESTERN_UNION, STARBUCKS_KIOSK, SUSHI_BAR, FLORAL_CUSTOM, EBT_SNAP_WIC, LOTTERY, MONEY_ORDER, BOTTLE_RETURN).']
  capability_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the service capability, including what it offers and any special conditions or limitations.']
  capability_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the service capability for customer-facing display and internal reference.']
  capacity_limit varchar [note: 'Maximum concurrent capacity for this service capability (e.g., maximum number of pickup orders that can be processed simultaneously, number of drive-thru lanes, kiosk stations). Null if no specific limit applies.']
  capacity_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the capacity limit (e.g., orders per hour, concurrent customers, transaction slots).']
  compliance_certification_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether staff must complete specific compliance training or certification to operate this service capability (e.g., HIPAA training for pharmacy, food safety certification for food service).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this service capability record was first created in the system.']
  customer_facing_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this service capability should be displayed to customers in store locator tools, mobile apps, and marketing materials.']
  daily_capacity_limit varchar [note: 'Maximum number of service transactions or orders that can be fulfilled in a single day. Used for omnichannel order routing and capacity planning.']
  display_priority varchar [note: 'Numeric priority for ordering service capabilities in customer-facing displays. Lower numbers indicate higher priority.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this service capability was or will be discontinued at the store location. Null indicates the service is ongoing with no planned end date.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this service capability became or will become available at the store location.']
  equipment_description varchar [note: 'Description of the specialized equipment or infrastructure required to support this service capability, including make, model, or configuration details.']
  equipment_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether specialized equipment or infrastructure is required to deliver this service capability (e.g., refrigerated pickup lockers, drive-thru window, kiosk hardware).']
  installation_date date [note: 'Date when the equipment or infrastructure for this service capability was installed at the store location.']
  last_maintenance_date date [note: 'Date when the most recent maintenance or inspection was performed on the equipment or infrastructure supporting this service capability.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this service capability record was most recently modified.']
  license_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the regulatory license or permit expires and must be renewed to continue offering this service capability.']
  license_number varchar [note: 'Regulatory license or permit number authorizing the store to offer this service capability. Confidential business information.']
  license_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a regulatory license or permit is required to offer this service capability (e.g., pharmacy license, lottery license, money transmitter license).']
  next_maintenance_due_date date [note: 'Date when the next scheduled maintenance or inspection is due for the equipment or infrastructure supporting this service capability.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form notes or comments about this service capability, including special instructions, known issues, or temporary conditions.']
  omnichannel_routing_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this service capability is integrated with the Order Management System (OMS) for automated omnichannel order routing and fulfillment assignment.']
  oms_capability_code varchar [note: 'Code used by the Order Management System to identify and route orders to this service capability. Must match OMS configuration.']
  operating_hours_description varchar [note: 'Textual description of the operating hours for this service capability if different from store hours (e.g., Pharmacy: Mon-Fri 9am-9pm, Sat 9am-6pm, Sun 10am-6pm).']
  operating_hours_same_as_store_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this service capability operates during the same hours as the store itself, or has different operating hours.']
  revenue_share_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of revenue from this service capability that is shared with a vendor partner. Confidential business information used for financial reporting and vendor management.']
  seasonal_end_month varchar [note: 'Month number (1-12) when seasonal service capability typically ends each year. Null if not seasonal.']
  seasonal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this service capability is offered seasonally rather than year-round (e.g., holiday gift wrapping, seasonal outdoor plant sales).']
  seasonal_start_month varchar [note: 'Month number (1-12) when seasonal service capability typically begins each year. Null if not seasonal.']
  staffing_requirement_fte decimal(5,2) [note: 'Number of full-time equivalent employees required to operate this service capability during standard operating hours. Used for labor planning and scheduling.']

  Note: 'Catalog of service capabilities available at a store location, such as Curbside Pickup, Home Delivery, Click-and-Collect, Pharmacy Drive-Through, Coinstar, Western Union, Starbucks Kiosk, Sushi Bar, Floral Custom Orders, EBT/SNAP/WIC acceptance, lottery sales, money orders, and bottle return. Each record defines the capability type, activation status, capacity limits (e.g., max concurrent pickup orders), staffing requirements, equipment dependencies, and effective date range. Enables omnichannel order routing, customer-facing store locator feature display, labor planning, and site selection analysis.'
}

Table store.mfc_profile {
  mfc_profile_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the micro-fulfillment center profile record. Primary key.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Micro-fulfillment centers are capital-intensive operations with dedicated cost centers for robotics labor, maintenance, and fulfillment costs — separate from the host store\'s cost center. Grocery reta']
  gateway_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to payment.gateway. Business justification: MFC profiles process ecommerce/omnichannel orders through dedicated payment gateways distinct from the store\'s POS gateway. Grocery retailers route MFC fulfillment transactions through separate ecomme']
  node_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fulfillment.node. Business justification: MFC operational management requires linking each in-store micro-fulfillment center profile to its corresponding fulfillment node for order routing, capacity planning, and WMS integration. A retail-gro']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: MFCs in large grocery chains are tracked as distinct profit centers separate from the host store to measure e-commerce fulfillment P&L. Grocery retailers report MFC contribution margin separately for ']
  sla_policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fulfillment.sla_policy. Business justification: Each MFC operates under specific SLA commitments (e.g., 2-hour pickup windows). Linking mfc_profile to sla_policy enables MFC-level SLA configuration, breach monitoring, and auto-compensation rules. R']
  store_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the retail store location with which this MFC is co-located or adjacent. Links the MFC to its parent store for operational and reporting purposes.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: Robotics vendor contracts are managed per fulfillment center; linking enables tracking of vendor performance, costs, and compliance for each store\'s MFC.']
  supplier_site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier_site. Business justification: MFC replenishment is sourced from a specific supplier site (vendor warehouse or distribution point). Grocery retailers operationally track which supplier site feeds each MFC for cold-chain compliance,']
  activation_date date [note: 'Date when the MFC became operational and began fulfilling customer orders. Used for capacity planning and performance tracking.']
  ambient_zone_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the MFC has an ambient temperature zone for storing shelf-stable products (dry goods, canned items, non-perishables).']
  average_inventory_accuracy_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Rolling average percentage of inventory records that match physical counts during cycle counts. Key metric for operational excellence.']
  average_order_accuracy_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Rolling average percentage of orders fulfilled without errors (wrong items, missing items, damaged items). Critical customer satisfaction metric.']
  average_order_fulfillment_time_minutes decimal(8,2) [note: 'Mean time in minutes from order receipt to order ready for pickup or dispatch. Critical SLA metric for omnichannel fulfillment.']
  average_pick_time_seconds decimal(8,2) [note: 'Mean time in seconds to pick a single item from inventory. Key performance indicator for MFC efficiency and throughput modeling.']
  chilled_zone_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the MFC has a refrigerated zone for storing chilled products (dairy, fresh produce, deli items) typically maintained at 32-40°F.']
  cold_chain_monitoring_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the MFC has automated temperature monitoring systems for chilled and frozen zones to ensure cold chain integrity.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this MFC profile record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  daily_order_capacity varchar [note: 'Maximum number of customer orders the MFC can fulfill in a 24-hour period under normal operating conditions. Used for order routing and capacity planning.']
  deactivation_date date [note: 'Date when the MFC ceased operations, if applicable. Null for active MFCs. Used for historical analysis and capacity planning.']
  food_safety_inspection_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numerical score from the most recent food safety inspection, typically on a 0-100 scale. Higher scores indicate better compliance.']
  frozen_zone_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the MFC has a frozen storage zone for frozen foods, ice cream, and other products requiring temperatures at or below 0°F.']
  fulfillment_service_types varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of fulfillment services supported by this MFC (e.g., curbside pickup, home delivery, ship-from-store, same-day delivery). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: curbside_pickup|home_delivery|ship_from_store|same_day_delivery|next_day_delivery|scheduled_delivery|locker_pickup — promote to reference product]']
  haccp_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the MFC has achieved HACCP certification for food safety management, required for facilities handling perishable foods.']
  last_food_safety_inspection_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent food safety inspection by regulatory authorities (FDA, USDA, or state health department). Used for compliance tracking.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this MFC profile record was most recently updated. Used for change tracking and audit purposes.']
  mfc_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the MFC, used in operational systems and reporting. Format: MFC followed by 6 digits.']
  mfc_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or designation of the MFC, typically including geographic or store reference for easy identification by operations teams.']
  mfc_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the MFC based on its operational model: automated (fully robotic picking and packing), semi-automated (hybrid human and robotic), or manual (human-only fulfillment).']
  number_of_packing_stations varchar [note: 'Count of workstations where picked orders are packed into bags or boxes for customer delivery or pickup. Used for labor planning.']
  number_of_picking_stations varchar [note: 'Count of workstations where associates or robots pick items into customer orders. Key metric for throughput capacity planning.']
  number_of_robots varchar [note: 'Total count of robotic units (picking robots, transport robots, sorting robots) deployed in the MFC. Zero for manual facilities.']
  oms_integration_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the MFC is integrated with the enterprise Order Management System for real-time order routing and fulfillment orchestration.']
  operating_hours_end varchar [note: 'Daily end time for MFC operations in 24-hour HH:MM format. Used for order routing and labor scheduling.']
  operating_hours_start varchar [note: 'Daily start time for MFC operations in 24-hour HH:MM format. Used for order routing and labor scheduling.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the MFC indicating whether it is actively fulfilling orders, temporarily offline, being built, or permanently closed.']
  peak_hourly_order_capacity varchar [note: 'Maximum number of orders the MFC can process in a single hour during peak demand periods. Critical for real-time order routing decisions.']
  picking_square_footage decimal(10,2) [note: 'Floor area dedicated to order picking operations in square feet. Used for productivity analysis and space utilization metrics.']
  rfid_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the MFC uses RFID technology for inventory tracking and order accuracy verification.']
  robotics_vendor varchar [note: 'Name of the robotics or automation technology provider for automated or semi-automated MFCs (e.g., AutoStore, Dematic, Ocado). Null for manual MFCs.']
  sku_count varchar [note: 'Total number of unique SKUs stocked in the MFC at any given time. Indicates assortment breadth and inventory complexity.']
  total_square_footage decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total floor area of the MFC facility in square feet, including all operational zones, staging areas, and support spaces.']
  total_tote_capacity varchar [note: 'Maximum number of totes (storage containers) that the MFC can hold at any given time. Key metric for inventory capacity planning.']
  twenty_four_hour_operation boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the MFC operates continuously 24 hours per day, 7 days per week. True for round-the-clock facilities.']
  wms_instance_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the specific WMS instance or tenant serving this MFC. Used for technical integration and troubleshooting.']
  wms_system_name varchar [note: 'Name of the WMS platform integrated with this MFC (e.g., Manhattan Associates, Blue Yonder, SAP EWM). Used for system integration and support.']

  Note: 'Master profile for a Micro-Fulfillment Center (MFC) co-located with or adjacent to a store. Captures MFC ID, associated store location, MFC type (automated/manual), total tote capacity, daily order throughput capacity, temperature zone configuration (ambient, chilled, frozen), WMS system integration reference, activation date, and operational status. Supports omnichannel fulfillment routing and capacity planning.'
}

Table store.health_inspection {
  health_inspection_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the health inspection record. Primary key for the health inspection entity.']
  store_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the store location where the health inspection was conducted.']
  closure_end_date date [note: 'Date when the store or department was permitted to reopen after corrective actions were verified and approved by the inspecting agency.']
  closure_ordered boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the inspecting agency ordered immediate closure of the store or specific departments due to critical food safety violations posing imminent health hazard. True indicates closure ordered, False indicates no closure.']
  closure_start_date date [note: 'Date when the store or department closure became effective due to health violations.']
  corrective_action_due_date date [note: 'Date by which all corrective actions must be completed to address violations identified during the inspection.']
  corrective_action_plan varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the corrective actions required to address violations, including specific steps, responsible parties, and timelines for remediation.']
  corrective_action_required boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether corrective actions are required to address violations identified during the inspection. True indicates corrective actions needed, False indicates no actions required.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the health inspection record was first created in the system.']
  critical_violation_count varchar [note: 'Number of critical violations identified during the inspection. Critical violations are food safety issues that pose immediate risk to public health (e.g., improper food temperatures, cross-contamination, inadequate handwashing).']
  cross_contamination_compliance varchar [note: 'Compliance status for preventing cross-contamination between raw and ready-to-eat foods, proper separation of food preparation areas, and equipment sanitation.']
  departments_inspected varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of store departments included in the inspection scope (e.g., Deli, Bakery, Meat, Seafood, Produce, Pharmacy, Prepared Foods, Grocery).']
  facility_maintenance_compliance varchar [note: 'Compliance status for physical facility maintenance including floors, walls, ceilings, lighting, ventilation, and equipment condition.']
  food_temperature_compliance varchar [note: 'Compliance status for proper food storage and holding temperatures. Critical for preventing bacterial growth and foodborne illness.']
  haccp_compliance_status varchar [note: 'Status of compliance with HACCP food safety management system principles. HACCP is a systematic preventive approach to food safety addressing biological, chemical, and physical hazards. Compliant indicates full adherence, non-compliant indicates violations, not-applicable indicates HACCP not required for this location, partial indicates some HACCP elements in place.']
  handwashing_compliance varchar [note: 'Compliance status for proper handwashing facilities, employee hand hygiene practices, and personal cleanliness standards.']
  inspecting_agency varchar [note: 'Name of the regulatory agency or health department that conducted the inspection (e.g., County Health Department, FDA, USDA).']
  inspection_date date [note: 'Date when the health inspection was conducted at the store location.']
  inspection_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Total duration of the inspection in minutes, from start to completion.']
  inspection_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique inspection number assigned by the inspecting agency or internal tracking system.']
  inspection_report_url varchar [note: 'URL link to the full inspection report document stored in the document management system or public health department website.']
  inspection_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the health inspection. Scheduled indicates future inspection, in-progress indicates active inspection, completed indicates inspection finished, passed indicates no critical violations, failed indicates critical violations found, pending-review indicates awaiting final determination, cancelled indicates inspection did not occur. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: scheduled|in-progress|completed|passed|failed|pending-review|cancelled — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  inspection_type varchar [note: 'Type of health inspection conducted. Routine inspections are scheduled periodic reviews, follow-up inspections verify corrective actions, complaint-driven inspections respond to customer or employee reports, pre-opening inspections occur before new store launch, special inspections address specific concerns, and reinspections verify compliance after violations.']
  inspector_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier or badge number of the health inspector assigned by the inspecting agency.']
  inspector_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the health inspector who conducted the inspection.']
  letter_grade varchar [note: 'Letter grade assigned to the inspection based on overall score, if applicable in the jurisdiction (A=excellent, B=good, C=satisfactory, D=marginal, F=failing).']
  non_critical_violation_count varchar [note: 'Number of non-critical violations identified during the inspection. Non-critical violations are food safety issues that do not pose immediate risk but require correction (e.g., minor equipment maintenance, documentation gaps).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, observations, or comments from the inspector or store management regarding the inspection, violations, or corrective actions.']
  overall_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numerical score assigned to the inspection representing overall food safety compliance. Scoring scale varies by jurisdiction (e.g., 0-100 scale where higher is better, or deduction-based scoring).']
  pest_control_compliance varchar [note: 'Compliance status for pest prevention and control measures, including evidence of rodents, insects, or other pests.']
  posted_at_store boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the inspection results or grade card has been posted at the store location as required by local regulations. True indicates posted, False indicates not posted.']
  public_disclosure_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the inspection results are subject to public disclosure or posting requirements per local health department regulations. True indicates public disclosure required, False indicates confidential.']
  reinspection_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for follow-up reinspection to verify that corrective actions have been completed and violations have been resolved.']
  reinspection_required boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether a follow-up reinspection is required to verify corrective actions have been completed. True indicates reinspection needed, False indicates no reinspection required.']
  score_scale varchar [note: 'Description of the scoring scale used for the overall score (e.g., 0-100 scale, A-F letter grade, pass/fail, deduction-based).']
  total_violation_count varchar [note: 'Total number of violations (critical and non-critical combined) identified during the inspection.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the health inspection record was last modified in the system.']

  Note: 'Record of food safety and health department inspections conducted at a store location. Captures inspection ID, inspection date, inspecting agency (local health department, FDA, USDA), inspection type (routine, follow-up, complaint-driven), overall score, critical violation count, non-critical violation count, HACCP compliance status, corrective action plan, and re-inspection date. Supports FDA/USDA regulatory compliance and HACCP program management.'
}

Table store.lease {
  lease_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for lease']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Grocery finance teams track occupancy expense by cost center for store-level profitability analys']

Business justification: ASC 842/IFRS 16 lease accounting requires each lease mapped to specific GL accounts (right-of-use asset, lease liability, rent expense). Grocery retailers with hundreds of leased locations must post l']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Leases are executed by a specific legal entity (the lessee). Multi-banner grocery operators (Kroger, Albertsons) have different legal entities signing leases per banner/region. SOX disclosure flag on ']
  store_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the store location covered by this lease agreement.']
  base_rent_amount_usd decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monthly or annual base rent amount in US dollars before additional charges.']
  base_rent_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which base rent payments are due.']
  cam_charges_usd decimal(15,2) [note: 'Common Area Maintenance charges in US dollars covering shared facility costs such as landscaping, parking lot maintenance, and common utilities.']
  co_tenancy_clause_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the lease includes a co-tenancy clause that provides rent relief or termination rights if anchor tenants vacate the property.']
  commencement_date date [note: 'Date when the lease agreement becomes effective and rent obligations begin.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this lease record was first created in the system.']
  document_url varchar [note: 'URL or file path to the digitally stored lease agreement document for reference and audit purposes.']
  early_termination_allowed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the lease agreement includes an early termination clause allowing tenant to exit before expiration date.']
  early_termination_penalty_usd decimal(15,2) [note: 'Financial penalty amount in US dollars that tenant must pay if exercising early termination clause.']
  exclusivity_category varchar [note: 'Business category or industry segment protected under the exclusivity provision (e.g., grocery, pharmacy, fuel).']
  exclusivity_provision_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the lease includes an exclusivity clause preventing landlord from leasing to competing businesses in the same property.']
  execution_date date [note: 'Date when the lease agreement was signed by both landlord and tenant.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the lease agreement term ends unless renewed or extended.']
  guarantor_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the entity or individual providing financial guarantee for the lease obligations.']
  insurance_amount_usd decimal(15,2) [note: 'Annual insurance premium amount in US dollars that tenant is responsible for under the lease terms.']

  landlord_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for landlord contact. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  landlord_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the landlord or property owner entity that holds title to the leased premises.']
  landlord_phone varchar [note: 'Primary phone number for landlord contact. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  lease_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known lease agreement number or reference code used by landlord and tenant for identification.']
  lease_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the lease agreement.']
  lease_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the lease agreement type for accounting and operational purposes.']
  leased_square_footage decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total square footage of the leased premises as specified in the lease agreement.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form text field for additional lease terms, special conditions, or administrative notes.']
  property_address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the leased property street address. Organizational location data classified as confidential.']
  property_address_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the leased property street address (suite, unit, building). Organizational location data classified as confidential.']
  property_city varchar [note: 'City where the leased property is located. Organizational location data classified as confidential.']
  property_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code where the leased property is located.']
  property_postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code of the leased property. Organizational location data classified as confidential.']
  property_state_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter state or province code where the leased property is located. Organizational location data classified as confidential.']
  property_tax_amount_usd decimal(15,2) [note: 'Annual property tax amount in US dollars that tenant is responsible for under the lease terms.']
  renewal_notice_days varchar [note: 'Number of days prior to lease expiration that tenant must provide written notice to exercise renewal option.']
  renewal_option_count varchar [note: 'Number of renewal option periods available to the tenant after the initial lease term expires.']
  renewal_option_term_months varchar [note: 'Duration in months of each renewal option period if exercised by the tenant.']
  rent_escalation_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Annual percentage rate at which base rent increases if escalation type is percentage-based.']
  rent_escalation_type varchar [note: 'Type of rent escalation clause defining how base rent increases over the lease term.']
  security_deposit_usd decimal(15,2) [note: 'Security deposit amount in US dollars held by landlord to cover potential damages or unpaid rent.']
  sox_disclosure_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this lease agreement requires disclosure in financial statements under Sarbanes-Oxley Act compliance requirements.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this lease record was last modified in the system.']

  Note: 'Real estate lease record for store locations that are leased rather than owned. Captures lease ID, store location, landlord entity name, lease commencement date, lease expiration date, renewal option terms, base rent amount, CAM (Common Area Maintenance) charges, rent escalation schedule, co-tenancy clauses, exclusivity provisions, and lease status. Supports real estate portfolio management, SOX financial disclosure, and capital vs. operating lease classification.'
}

Table inventory.stock_position {

Linking stock_position to assortment_item enables on-shelf availability vs. assortment plan report']
  category_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to assortment.category. Business justification: Category‑Level Stock Position Reporting requires each stock position to be tied to its product category for aggregated inventory metrics.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Holding cost allocation ties each stock position to a cost center for budgeting and profitability analysis per store.']
  department_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to store.department. Business justification: Stock position is physically located within a store department; linking enables department‑level inventory visibility.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Stock positions represent on-hand inventory value mapped to balance sheet inventory asset GL accounts. Grocery retailers require stock_position→gl_account for inventory valuation reporting, balance sh']
  markdown_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.markdown. Business justification: Active markdown inventory tracking: stock_position must reference any active markdown to support sell-through rate monitoring and markdown effectiveness reporting. Retail-grocery category managers tra']
  pharmacy_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pharmacy.pharmacy_location. Business justification: PHARMACY STOCK AUDIT: ties each stock position to the pharmacy location for drug inventory compliance and daily stock‑reconciliation reports.']
  planogram_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to assortment.planogram. Business justification: Planogram compliance reporting — comparing actual on-shelf stock positions against planned positions — is a core grocery retail KPI. On-shelf availability dashboards require linking stock positions to']
  promo_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to promotion.promo_campaign. Business justification: Promotional Allocation: inventory positions are earmarked for a campaign to ensure shelf space and display compliance.']
  reorder_policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.reorder_policy. Business justification: stock_position is the real-time inventory position at SKU-location level; reorder_policy defines the replenishment parameters for the same SKU-location combination. Adding reorder_policy_id FK links e']
  retail_price_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.retail_price. Business justification: Perishable clearance and markdown automation: stock_position tracks expiration_date and on_hand_quantity; linking to retail_price enables automated clearance pricing workflows and lost-sales dollar va']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Internal surrogate key referencing the product (SKU) whose inventory is tracked.']
  storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.storage_location. Business justification: Link stock_position to its physical storage location for precise inventory location tracking.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: PROCUREMENT PLANNING: Stock position reports need primary supplier to calculate lead times and order allocation per SKU.']
  tpr_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to promotion.tpr_event. Business justification: TPR inventory reservation and available-to-promise calculations require stock positions to reference the active TPR event. Retailers reserve inventory for TPR windows to prevent overselling; stock_pos']
  available_quantity decimal(12,2) [note: 'Units that can be sold to customers (on‑hand minus reserved).']
  cold_chain_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'True when temperature‑controlled items meet required temperature range.']
  estimated_lost_sales_value decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary estimate of sales lost due to the OOS condition.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date on which the product expires; applicable to perishable goods.']
  fifo_lifo_indicator varchar [note: 'Specifies whether the SKU is managed using First‑In‑First‑Out or Last‑In‑First‑Out rotation.']
  in_transit_quantity decimal(12,2) [note: 'Units currently being moved between locations (e.g., from DC to store) and not yet on‑hand.']
  is_perishable boolean [note: 'True if the SKU is a perishable item requiring special handling.']
  last_cycle_count_quantity decimal(12,2) [note: 'Quantity recorded during the most recent cycle count.']
  last_cycle_count_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent physical cycle count for this SKU at the location.']
  location_type varchar [note: 'Classifies the location as a retail store, distribution center (DC), or micro‑fulfillment center (MFC).']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Batch or lot identifier for traceability of perishable or regulated items.']
  on_hand_quantity decimal(12,2) [note: 'Current physical units of the SKU available on the shelf or floor at the location.']
  on_order_quantity decimal(12,2) [note: 'Units ordered from suppliers but not yet received at any location.']
  oos_detection_method varchar [note: 'Technique by which the OOS condition was identified.']
  oos_duration_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total duration of the OOS event expressed in hours.']
  oos_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the SKU was restocked and the OOS condition ended (null if still OOS).']
  oos_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the SKU is currently out of stock at the location.']
  oos_root_cause varchar [note: 'Categorized reason for the OOS event.']
  oos_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the out‑of‑stock condition began.']
  perishable_category varchar [note: 'Sub‑category of perishable items, used for handling and pricing rules.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when this stock position record was first created in the lakehouse.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent update to this stock position record.']
  reserved_quantity decimal(12,2) [note: 'Units allocated to open orders, transfers, or replenishment signals and therefore not sellable.']
  shrink_quantity decimal(12,2) [note: 'Units lost due to theft, spoilage, damage, or administrative error.']
  temperature_celsius decimal(5,2) [note: 'Most recent temperature reading for the location or pallet, in degrees Celsius.']
  temperature_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the temperature_celsius value was recorded.']
  upc_code varchar [note: '12‑digit barcode identifier for the product, used for POS scanning and inventory reconciliation.']

  Note: 'Real-time and near-real-time on-hand stock position for every SKU at every location (store, DC, MFC). Captures on-hand quantity, reserved quantity, available-to-sell quantity, in-transit quantity, on-order quantity, and comprehensive OOS tracking: OOS status flag, OOS start timestamp, OOS end timestamp (when restocked), OOS duration in hours, root cause category (supplier OOS, DC OOS, store receiving delay, shrink, demand spike), estimated lost sales value, and OOS detection method (POS scan failure, CAO signal, manual audit). This is the authoritative SSOT for current inventory levels and out-of-stock events across the enterprise, fed by SAP MM goods movements and Manhattan WMS inventory control. Supports OOS monitoring and alerting, CAO replenishment signal generation, omnichannel availability checks, and store operations performance measurement.'
}

Table inventory.goods_movement {
  goods_movement_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each inventory movement event.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Internal movements are charged to a cost center to track transfer costs and support internal charge‑back reporting.']
  cost_price_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.cost_price. Business justification: COGS and inventory valuation audit: goods_movement records every stock transaction with cost_at_movement; linking to cost_price enables cost variance analysis between standard cost and actual movement']
  department_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to store.department. Business justification: Goods movement often occurs between departments; linking provides departmental movement tracking.']
  destination_location_storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Location (store, DC, MFC) to which inventory is added.']
  fiscal_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: Inventory movements must be period-stamped for COGS calculation, period-end inventory valuation, and financial close. Grocery retailers associate goods movements with fiscal periods to prevent cross-p']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Every inventory goods movement (receipt, adjustment, write-off, transfer) generates a GL posting in grocery ERP systems. goods_movement→gl_account is required for inventory valuation, COGS posting, an']
  goods_receipt_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.goods_receipt. Business justification: goods_receipt (supply-side) triggers goods_movement (inventory-side) — this is the fundamental GR/GI posting in retail ERP systems. The goods_movement records the inventory impact of a goods_receipt. ']
  lot_batch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.lot_batch. Business justification: goods_movement has denormalized `lot_number` (STRING) and `batch_number` (STRING) attributes that are natural keys for the lot_batch master record. Adding lot_batch_id FK normalizes these references t']
  primary_goods_storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Location (store, DC, MFC) from which inventory is removed.']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.purchase_order. Business justification: REQUIRED: Goods movement (inbound/outbound) is audited against the PO that generated the movement for traceability and audit.']
  receiving_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.receiving_record. Business justification: goods_movement records receipt-type movements (movement_type = \'RECEIPT\', is_physical_inventory = true). receiving_record is the formal inbound receiving document. Adding receiving_record_id FK links ']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Inventory valuation, shrink reporting, and goods movement audit trails in grocery are produced at SKU level. goods_movement has product_item_id but not sku_id. Movements involving variable-weight or c']
  stock_position_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_position. Business justification: Every goods_movement event affects a specific stock position (on_hand_quantity changes with every receipt, issue, transfer, or adjustment). Adding stock_position_id FK links each movement transaction ']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: TRACEABILITY: Inbound goods movements must capture the supplying vendor for compliance and supplier performance audits.']
  transfer_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.transfer_order. Business justification: goods_movement records inventory movement events including inter-location transfers. transfer_order is the authorizing document for such movements. Adding transfer_order_id FK links movement transacti']
  vendor_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.vendor_item. Business justification: Goods movements require vendor_item for accurate cost-at-movement (vendor_cost_amount), unit-of-measure validation, and COGS posting. Inventory valuation and shrink costing depend on the specific vend']
  wms_task_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fulfillment.wms_task. Business justification: Every WMS task execution (pick, putaway, transfer) generates a goods_movement record in inventory. Linking goods_movement to the originating wms_task enables inventory discrepancy reconciliation, shri']
  adjustment_reason varchar [note: 'Free‑text or coded reason for an inventory adjustment (e.g., shrink, recount, donation).']
  cost_at_movement decimal(18,4) [note: 'Standard cost of the inventory at the time of movement, used for financial valuation.']
  cost_method varchar [note: 'Costing methodology applied to the inventory item at the time of movement.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the movement record was first created in the lakehouse.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the cost amount.']
  cycle_count_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quantity difference identified during a cycle count.']
  document_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the source document (e.g., material document number, WMS transaction ID).']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date on which the product expires; used for perishable inventory control.']
  goods_movement_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the movement record.']
  inventory_valuation_method varchar [note: 'Method used to value inventory for this movement (FIFO, LIFO, average cost, or standard cost).']
  is_cycle_count_correction boolean [note: 'True when the movement reflects a correction from a cycle‑count process.']
  is_manual_entry boolean [note: 'True if the movement was entered manually rather than via automated interface.']
  is_physical_inventory boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the movement is part of a physical inventory count process.']
  is_reserved boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the quantity was reserved for a pending order at the time of movement.']
  is_shrink boolean [note: 'True when the movement records inventory loss (theft, spoilage, damage).']
  movement_reason_code varchar [note: 'Code describing why the movement occurred (e.g., purchase receipt, customer return, internal transfer).']
  movement_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the inventory movement was posted in the source system.']
  movement_type varchar [note: 'Category of inventory movement: receipt, issue, transfer, adjustment, correction, or other.']
  quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Numeric amount of inventory moved, expressed in the unit of measure.']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'Unique serial number for serialized items (e.g., electronics, high‑value assets).']
  shrink_reason varchar [note: 'Categorized cause of shrinkage when is_shrink is true.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating operational system that generated the movement record.']
  temperature_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the movement complied with cold‑chain temperature requirements.']
  temperature_reading decimal(6,2) [note: 'Recorded temperature at the time of movement for temperature‑sensitive items.']
  temperature_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of the temperature reading (Celsius, Fahrenheit, Kelvin).']
  transaction_document_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable document number shown on transaction prints and reports.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Standard unit used for the quantity (e.g., each, kilogram, pound, case, pallet).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the movement record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of every inventory movement event — receipts, issues, transfers, adjustments (including cycle-count corrections, damage write-offs, donation dispositions, and recall removals), returns, and write-offs — across all locations. Each record captures movement type (GR, GI, transfer, adjustment, cycle-count-correction), quantity, unit of measure, originating document reference, posting timestamp, and for adjustments: reason code, approving manager, adjustment value at cost, and supporting documentation reference. Sourced from SAP MM material documents and Manhattan WMS transactions. Provides the full audit trail for stock position changes, supports FIFO/LIFO rotation tracking, and serves as the controlled, auditable record for all inventory quantity changes including authorized corrections. This is the single source of truth for all inventory quantity change events.'
}

Table inventory.shrink_record {
  shrink_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each shrink record.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. The responsible_department on shrink_record must map to a cost_cent']
  department_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to store.department. Business justification: Shrink is budgeted, tracked, and reported at the department level in grocery. Department managers own shrink reduction programs and are accountable to shrink targets. responsible_department is a denor']
  fiscal_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.fiscal_period. Business justification: Shrink must be reported by fiscal period for P&L shrink rate KPIs, budget variance analysis, and regulatory reporting. Grocery retailers track shrink as a percentage of sales by period; shrink_record→']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Shrink write-downs (spoilage, theft, damage) must be posted to specific GL shrink-expense or waste accounts for P&L reporting. Grocery retailers require shrink_record→gl_account to drive accurate inve']
  goods_movement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.goods_movement. Business justification: shrink events generate inventory adjustment goods_movements (goods_movement.is_shrink = true). Adding goods_movement_id FK on shrink_record links each shrink event to its corresponding inventory adjus']
  lot_batch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.lot_batch. Business justification: shrink_record has a denormalized `lot_number` (STRING) attribute. Shrink events on perishable and regulated inventory must trace back to the lot_batch master for HACCP compliance and recall traceabili']
  markdown_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.markdown. Business justification: Perishable markdown-to-shrink traceability: markdowns that fail to clear inventory result in shrink disposal. Linking shrink_record to the triggering markdown event enables markdown effectiveness repo']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.purchase_order. Business justification: REQUIRED: Shrink analysis per PO enables supplier chargebacks, loss accounting, and regulatory reporting of inventory loss.']
  retail_price_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.retail_price. Business justification: Shrink dollar valuation (retail inventory method): shrink_record.estimated_cost requires the active retail price at time of loss. Retail-grocery loss-prevention and P&L reporting depend on this link. ']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Grocery shrink reporting and vendor chargeback processes require shrink records linked at SKU level. shrink_record has product_item_id but not sku_id. Shrink budgets, markdown decisions, and supplier ']
  stock_position_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_position. Business justification: Link shrink_record to the stock_position it originates from, consolidating shrink events with the affected position.']
  store_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the store, distribution center, or micro‑fulfillment center where the shrink occurred.']
  vendor_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.vendor_item. Business justification: Shrink records tied to vendor items enable vendor chargeback claims for damaged-on-arrival or short-shelf-life goods. Trade allowance recovery and vendor performance scorecarding for shrink contributi']
  compliance_flag_haccp boolean [note: 'Indicates if the shrink event relates to a HACCP (food safety) issue.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this shrink record was first created in the data lake.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the estimated cost.']
  disposal_method varchar [note: 'Method used to dispose of inventory that cannot be salvaged.']
  disposition_method varchar [note: 'How the lost inventory was handled after identification.']
  donation_recipient varchar [note: 'Name of the charitable organization or entity receiving the donated inventory.']
  estimated_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary value of the lost inventory based on standard cost.']
  line_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number of the line within the shrink event.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text for additional details or observations.']
  quantity_lost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Amount of inventory lost, expressed in the unit of measure.']
  recorded_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date‑time when the shrink event was captured in the source system.']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'Unique serial identifier for high‑value or regulated items.']
  shrink_category varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of the shrink event for reporting.']
  shrink_cause varchar [note: 'Root cause of the inventory loss.']
  shrink_event_number varchar [note: 'Identifier linking this line to the parent shrink event batch.']
  shrink_record_status varchar [note: 'Current processing state of the shrink record.']
  temperature_reading decimal(5,2) [note: 'Recorded temperature at the time of the event.']
  temperature_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the temperature reading.']
  temperature_violation boolean [note: 'True if temperature readings indicated a cold‑chain breach leading to spoilage.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Measurement unit for the quantity lost (e.g., each, kilogram, liter).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this shrink record.']

  Note: 'Operational record of inventory shrink events and loss-mitigation dispositions. Captures shrink cause (theft known/unknown, spoilage, damage, administrative error, vendor short-ship), quantity lost, estimated cost of shrink, shrink category, responsible department, and disposition method and outcome — including markdowns (clearance, near-expiry, seasonal, damage markdowns with original price, markdown price, markdown percentage, effective/expiry dates), donations, and disposal. For markdown dispositions: tracks quantity marked down, sell-through outcome, and recovered value. Supports GMROI analysis, loss prevention programs, HACCP compliance for perishable spoilage, shrink reduction programs, and perishable inventory management. Sourced from store shrink entry workflows in SAP MM and Oracle Retail.'
}

Table inventory.cycle_count {
  cycle_count_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each cycle count event.']
  department_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to store.department. Business justification: Cycle counts in grocery are scoped to departments (produce, deli, pharmacy). Department managers own cycle count schedules and variance accountability. Department-level inventory accuracy reporting an']
  goods_movement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.goods_movement. Business justification: cycle counts with variance generate inventory adjustment goods_movements (goods_movement.is_cycle_count_correction = true). Adding goods_movement_id FK on cycle_count links count events to their resul']
  lot_batch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.lot_batch. Business justification: cycle_count has a denormalized `lot_number` (STRING) attribute for lot-specific physical counts. Adding lot_batch_id FK links count events to the authoritative lot_batch master, enabling traceability ']
  planogram_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to assortment.planogram. Business justification: Grocery retailers schedule cycle counts by planogram reset cycle — counts are scoped to all SKUs within a planogram. Planogram-scoped cycle count scheduling and post-reset verification are standard re']
  product_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.product_item. Business justification: Cycle count variance reconciliation requires linking each count record to the product item master. Grocery inventory accuracy reporting (shrink budget, audit compliance) depends on joining cycle count']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Cycle count records carry sku as plain text — a denormalization of the SKU entity. Grocery inventory accuracy KPIs (variance by SKU, shrink by SKU) require a proper FK. Replacing the plain sku column ']
  slot_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fulfillment.slot_location. Business justification: In WMS-managed fulfillment centers, cycle counts are performed at specific pick slot locations. cycle_count already links to storage_location, but slot_location is the WMS-specific granularity for cyc']
  stock_position_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_position. Business justification: cycle_count verifies and updates stock positions. Adding stock_position_id FK links each count event to the stock position it verifies, enabling direct reconciliation between counted_quantity and on_h']
  storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific location (aisle, shelf, bin) within the store.']
  store_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to store.store_location. Business justification: Store operations teams schedule and execute cycle counts per store location. Store-level inventory accuracy reporting, shrink audits, and cycle count scheduling all require knowing which store a count']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or employee identifier of the associate who approved the count.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the count was approved.']
  count_method varchar [note: 'Method used for the count (full inventory, cycle count, spot check, or ad‑hoc).']
  count_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the count event, used for tracking and reference.']
  count_reason varchar [note: 'Business reason for initiating the count.']
  count_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the physical count was performed.']
  count_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the count was performed manually or by an automated system.']
  counted_quantity varchar [note: 'Physical quantity recorded by the associate during the count.']
  cycle_count_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the count event.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Expiration date of the product lot, if applicable.']
  is_out_of_stock boolean [note: 'True if the SKU is considered out‑of‑stock after the count.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments entered by the associate performing the count.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the cycle count record was first created in the system.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the cycle count record.']
  reserved_quantity varchar [note: 'Quantity of the SKU reserved for open orders at count time.']
  shrink_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the variance is classified as shrink (loss).']
  shrink_reason varchar [note: 'Root cause of shrink when shrink_flag is true.']
  system_quantity varchar [note: 'Quantity that the inventory system recorded for the SKU at the time of the count.']
  temperature_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'True if the counted item met cold‑chain temperature requirements.']
  temperature_reading_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Recorded temperature at the time of count for temperature‑sensitive items.']
  tolerance_flag boolean [note: 'True if the variance exceeds predefined tolerance thresholds.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Measurement unit used for the quantity (e.g., each, case, pallet, kilogram).']
  upc varchar [note: '12‑digit barcode number for the product.']
  variance_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage difference between counted and system quantities.']
  variance_quantity varchar [note: 'Difference between counted_quantity and system_quantity (counted – system).']
  variance_value decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary impact of the quantity variance based on standard cost.']

  Note: 'Scheduled and ad-hoc physical inventory count events at the SKU-location level. Captures count date, count method (full, cycle, spot), counted quantity, system quantity at time of count, variance quantity, variance value, count status (scheduled, in-progress, completed, approved), and the associate who performed the count. Drives inventory accuracy KPIs and triggers adjustment postings when variances exceed tolerance thresholds. Sourced from Manhattan WMS cycle count module and SAP MM physical inventory.'
}

Table inventory.replenishment_signal {
  replenishment_signal_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each replenishment signal record.']
  allocation_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.allocation_plan. Business justification: Replenishment signals are generated when stock falls below allocation_plan minimums. Supply planners need to know which allocation_plan drove a replenishment_signal to evaluate plan accuracy and adjus']
  category_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to assortment.category. Business justification: Category Replenishment Signal Aggregation groups signals by category to drive category‑wide ordering decisions.']
  department_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to store.department. Business justification: Replenishment in grocery is managed at the department level — department managers review signals, approve orders, and are accountable for in-stock rates. Department-level replenishment reporting and s']
  plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to assortment.assortment_plan. Business justification: Replenishment signals must align with the active assortment plan — signals should only be generated for items currently in the assortment. Merchandising-operations integration requires this link for a']
  planogram_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to assortment.planogram. Business justification: Planogram-driven replenishment — filling to planogram-specified facing counts and capacity — is a named grocery retail process. Replenishment signals must reference the active planogram to determine t']
  promo_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to promotion.promo_campaign. Business justification: Demand signal generated by an active campaign, used for automated replenishment planning.']
  reorder_policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.reorder_policy. Business justification: replenishment_signal records are generated by the CAO system based on reorder_policy parameters (reorder_point, safety_stock_qty, lead_time_days, etc.). Adding reorder_policy_id FK establishes the gov']
  replenishment_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.replenishment_order. Business justification: Replenishment signals trigger replenishment_order creation. Signal-to-order response time and fill rate are core supply chain KPIs. Supply planners need to track which replenishment_order was generate']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the SKU for which the signal is generated.']
  stock_position_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_position. Business justification: replenishment_signal is triggered by the stock position falling below reorder_point. Adding stock_position_id FK links each signal to the triggering stock position, enabling direct traceability from s']
  store_location_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the store, distribution center, or micro‑fulfillment center where the signal applies.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: AUTOMATED ORDERING: Replenishment signals must identify the designated supplier to generate correct purchase orders.']
  tpr_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to promotion.tpr_event. Business justification: TPR-driven replenishment is a distinct operational process: when a TPR is scheduled, the replenishment system generates pre-positioning signals specific to that price reduction event. replenishment_si']
  vendor_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to vendor.vendor_item. Business justification: Replenishment signals must validate suggested_order_quantity against vendor_item.minimum_order_quantity and order_multiple_quantity before generating a PO. Without this link, signals cannot be rounded']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the signal record was first persisted in the lakehouse.']
  demand_forecast_date date [note: 'Date for which the demand forecast quantity applies.']
  demand_forecast_units varchar [note: 'Projected units of demand for the SKU‑location over the forecast horizon that triggered the signal.']
  forecast_accuracy_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Historical accuracy of the demand forecast that generated this signal, expressed as a percentage.']
  inventory_on_hand varchar [note: 'Quantity of units physically available at the location at signal generation time.']
  inventory_reserved varchar [note: 'Units already allocated to existing orders or transfers and therefore unavailable for new demand.']
  inventory_shrink varchar [note: 'Units lost due to theft, spoilage, or damage recorded at the time of signal.']
  is_critical boolean [note: 'True if the signal requires immediate attention due to risk of stockout or high‑margin item.']
  is_oos boolean [note: 'True if the SKU is currently out of stock at the location.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Expected number of days from order placement to receipt of goods for the SKU at this location.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments entered by planners or system about the signal.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to the signal for planning and execution urgency.']
  reorder_point varchar [note: 'Inventory level at which the signal is triggered; when on‑hand falls below this value.']
  reorder_point_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the reorder point value was last calculated or updated.']
  replenishment_signal_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the signal: pending, converted to purchase order, or cancelled.']
  safety_stock_level varchar [note: 'Buffer stock quantity maintained to protect against demand variability and supply lead‑time.']
  signal_generated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the replenishment signal was created by the system.']
  signal_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the signal was automatically generated by the demand engine or manually overridden by a planner.']
  signal_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the signal record to support updates and re‑evaluations.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that produced the signal, e.g., Blue Yonder demand planning or Oracle Retail Replenishment.']
  suggested_order_quantity varchar [note: 'Quantity of units recommended to order to bring inventory back to target levels.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the signal record.']

  Note: 'CAO (Computer-Assisted Ordering) replenishment signals generated for each SKU-location combination when on-hand stock falls below reorder point or safety stock threshold. Captures signal type (auto-generated, manual override), suggested order quantity, reorder point at time of signal, safety stock level, lead time days, signal status (pending, converted-to-PO, cancelled), and the demand forecast that triggered the signal. Integrates Blue Yonder Demand Planning output with Oracle Retail Replenishment.'
}

Table inventory.oos_event {
  oos_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'System‑generated unique identifier for the OOS event record.']
  assortment_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to assortment.assortment_item. Business justification: OOS events drive assortment rationalization decisions — items with chronic OOS are candidates for delisting or supplier review. Category managers require OOS event history per assortment item for rang']
  department_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to store.department. Business justification: OOS events are department-specific in grocery — produce OOS differs from pharmacy OOS in root cause, accountability, and resolution. Department managers are measured on OOS rates. Department-level OOS']
  promo_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to promotion.promo_campaign. Business justification: Promotional OOS analysis and vendor deduction claims require direct campaign attribution on OOS events. Post-promo reporting tracks which campaigns caused stockouts; stock_position\'s promo_campaign_id']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Purchase order that was expected to deliver the SKU but contributed to the OOS.']
  replenishment_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.replenishment_order. Business justification: OOS events are resolved by replenishment_orders. OOS resolution time (time from oos_start to replenishment_order delivery) is a key retail KPI. Currently oos_event links to replenishment_signal but no']
  replenishment_signal_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the Computer‑Assisted Ordering (CAO) signal that triggered the OOS detection.']
  retail_price_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.retail_price. Business justification: Lost-sales revenue quantification: oos_event.estimated_lost_sales_value requires the active retail price to convert forecasted_demand_qty into dollar impact. Retail-grocery category managers and opera']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: OOS event tracking is a core retail KPI reported at SKU level (lost sales value, fill rate by SKU). The plain sku column is a denormalization. A proper sku_id FK enables OOS duration and lost-sales re']
  stock_position_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_position. Business justification: Link oos_event to the stock_position to associate out‑of‑stock events with the exact inventory record.']
  store_location_id varchar [note: 'Internal identifier of the specific location (store number, DC code, or MFC ID).']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the supplier responsible for the product when root cause is supplier OOS.']
  tpr_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to promotion.tpr_event. Business justification: TPR-driven stockout analysis is a named operational process: retailers track OOS events caused by TPR demand spikes for root cause reporting, vendor accountability, and future TPR inventory planning. ']
  actual_sales_qty decimal(10,2) [note: 'Number of units actually sold during the OOS period (typically zero).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the OOS event record was first created in the data lake.']
  detection_method varchar [note: 'How the OOS was identified: POS scan failure, CAO replenishment signal, or manual audit.']
  duration_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total duration of the out‑of‑stock event expressed in hours (difference between end and start timestamps).']
  estimated_lost_sales_value decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary estimate of sales lost due to the OOS condition, expressed in the store\'s currency.']
  forecasted_demand_qty decimal(10,2) [note: 'Projected quantity of the SKU expected to sell during the OOS window based on demand planning.']
  is_resolved boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the OOS event has been closed (true) or is still open (false).']
  location_type varchar [note: 'Type of location where the OOS occurred: store, distribution center (dc), or micro‑fulfillment center (mfc).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Optional free‑text comments providing additional context or observations about the OOS event.']
  oos_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the item was replenished and the OOS condition ended.']
  oos_severity varchar [note: 'Business impact severity of the OOS event based on expected sales loss and product importance.']
  oos_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the inventory quantity first reached zero and the OOS event began.']
  quantity_after varchar [note: 'On‑hand quantity of the SKU after the OOS was resolved (post‑replenishment).']
  quantity_before varchar [note: 'On‑hand quantity of the SKU immediately before it reached zero.']
  root_cause_category varchar [note: 'Primary reason for the OOS event, classified into standard categories.']
  shrink_type varchar [note: 'Category of inventory loss if the OOS root cause is shrinkage.']
  temperature_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'True if temperature‑controlled items remained within required range during the OOS period; otherwise false.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent update to the OOS event record.']

  Note: 'Out-of-Stock (OOS) event records capturing when a SKU at a specific location transitions to zero available-to-sell quantity. Tracks OOS start timestamp, OOS end timestamp (when restocked), OOS duration in hours, root cause category (supplier OOS, DC OOS, store receiving delay, shrink, demand spike), estimated lost sales value, and whether the OOS was detected via POS scan failure, CAO signal, or manual audit. Critical for store operations performance and vendor fill-rate accountability.'
}

Vendor chargebacks for quality failures require identifying the PO under which a defective lo']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Lot/batch rotation and recall operations in grocery are executed at SKU level. product_sku is a plain-text denormalization of the SKU entity. Replacing it with a proper sku_id FK enables SKU-level lot']
  storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the warehouse, store, or micro‑fulfillment center where the lot is stored.']
  store_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to store.store_location. Business justification: FDA/USDA product recall traceability requires knowing which store received each lot/batch. Store-level lot tracking is mandatory for food safety compliance, health inspection documentation, and target']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the supplier that provided the lot.']

  batch_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer‑assigned batch code used for traceability across the supply chain.']

  country_of_origin varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code indicating where the product was produced.']
  days_to_expiry varchar [note: 'Calculated number of days remaining until the expiration date at the time of check.']
  entry_temperature_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Temperature recorded when the lot entered the cold chain.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date after which the product must not be sold or consumed.']
  lot_batch_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the lot within inventory management.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable identifier assigned to the lot by the manufacturer or supplier.']
  lot_type varchar [note: 'Category describing the nature of the lot for handling and compliance purposes.']
  production_date date [note: 'Date the product was manufactured or produced.']
  quantity decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total amount of product units contained in the lot.']
  quarantine_end_date date [note: 'Date the lot exited quarantine status.']
  quarantine_start_date date [note: 'Date the lot entered quarantine status.']
  recall_date date [note: 'Date the lot was officially recalled from the supply chain.']
